title: refactor(shared): delete dead modules/exports and inline single-caller billing helpers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Delete dead files: shared/utils.ts (omit/safeRound duplicated per-service) and shared/registry/types.ts (EventType duplicated generation-event.ts; repointed model.ts import)
- Drop dead exports: isValidModel, MARKUP_PERCENT, AudioVoice (shared/registry/text), getTierEmoji/getTierColor/TIER_COLORS + per-tier color field; un-export internal-only isVideoService, expandIPv6, getTextModels/getImageModels/getAudioModels
- balance.ts: trim re-export barrel to UserBalance only; inline determineBalanceSource into createBalanceCheckResult and drop the unused BalanceSource type
- deduction.ts: collapse atomicAdjustUserBalance into atomicCreditUserBalance (only ever credited positives); delete getUserBalances (test-only duplicate of balance.ts getUserBalance)
- Migrate 3 test files from getUserBalances to getUserBalance

Risks: none — pure dead-code removal and single-caller inlining; no callers or behavior change. balance/deduction logic verified by gen byop-markup+billing-deduction (22 tests) and enter tier-system (8 tests); gen typecheck clean; enter typecheck unchanged from main (79 pre-existing @pollinations/ui frontend errors, none in touched files).

_~196 net lines removed · biome ✓ · gen.pollinations.ai vitest: src/byop-markup.test.ts (17) + test/billing-deduction.test.ts  ✓_

gist.headline: Trimming the Weeds
gist.blurb: We did some spring cleaning in our shared codebase, removing dead code and streamlining our billing helpers. A lighter codebase means a healthier hive!
gist.summary: Removed approximately 196 lines of dead code from the shared modules, including unused billing helpers, redundant types, and obsolete exports like MARKUP_PERCENT and AudioVoice.
gist.impact: This internal refactor simplifies the codebase and reduces technical debt without changing any user-facing behavior.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a gardener, pruning dead code (leaves) from a plant, while the Nomnom creature eats the discarded code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's apron, pruning dead pixelated leaves off a glowing lime green (#ecf874) code-plant. The round tan nomnom creature sits below, happily eating the fallen dead code leaves with a 'NOMNOM' speech bubble. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
